name: Hoces del Cabriel and Contreras Reservoir Loop
name_original: Embalse de Contreras — circular desde Enguídanos
description: This difficult 83.1-mile road cycling route from Enguídanos offers stunning views of Embalse de Contreras and varied terrain.
region_intro: Road cycling around Enguídanos, located in the province of Cuenca, offers routes through the mountainous terrain of the Serranía Baja and the Cabriel River Valley. The region is characterized by dramatic gorges, lush riverside landscapes, and a mix of pine forests, olive groves, and almond trees. Cyclists can expect varied elevation changes, providing a challenging yet rewarding experience across mostly well-paved surfaces. The low population density contributes to a tranquil cycling environment with minimal car traffic. Best road cycling routes around Enguídanos The most popular road cycling route is Embalse de Contreras — circular desde Enguídanos, an 83.1 miles (133.8 km) difficult trail that takes 6 hours 33 minutes to complete. This challenging route features substantial elevation gain as it circles the Embalse de Contreras. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puertecillo de Yemeda – Enguídanos loop from Área recreativa La Playeta de Villora, a difficult 51.4 miles (82.8 km) path. This loop offers extensive views of the regional landscape, including river valleys and forested areas. Local road cyclists also love the Monasterio de Tejeda – La Loberuela loop from Narboneta, a 49.6 miles (79.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant climbs, often completed in about 3 hours 54 minutes. Road cycling around Enguídanos is defined by its river valleys, mountainous terrain, and extensive pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging routes with considerable elevation. The routes in Enguídanos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Enguídanos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Enguídanos?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Enguídanos, offering a variety of distances and elevation gains. These routes explore the mountainous terrain of the Serranía Baja and the scenic Cabriel River Valley.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Enguídanos?
A: Road cycling in Enguídanos features varied terrain, from dramatic gorges and lush riverside landscapes to pine forests, olive groves, and almond trees. You can expect significant elevation changes, providing a challenging yet rewarding experience across mostly well-paved surfaces. The region's low population density also means minimal car traffic for a tranquil ride.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Enguídanos is known for its challenging routes with substantial elevation gain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1373964560/embalse-de-contreras-circular-desde-enguidanos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Contreras — circular desde Enguídanos&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 83.1-mile route with over 1,800 meters of climbing. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24538100" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertecillo de Yemeda – Enguídanos loop from Área recreativa La Playeta de Villora&lt;/a&gt;, a 51.4-mile difficult path with extensive views.

Q: Can I find any moderate road cycling options in the area?
A: While many routes are challenging, there are moderate options available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38157687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Venta del Moro loop from Mira&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 42.5-mile route with over 550 meters of elevation gain, offering a less strenuous but still scenic ride.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling around Enguídanos?
A: You'll encounter diverse natural beauty, including the clean, turquoise waters of the Cabriel River, dramatic gorges like Hoz del Agua, and extensive pine forests. Routes often pass by significant landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6098023" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;village of Enguídanos&lt;/a&gt; itself, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paracuellos Castle&lt;/a&gt;, and offer views of the Embalse de Contreras reservoir.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Enguídanos are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1373964560/embalse-de-contreras-circular-desde-enguidanos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Contreras — circular desde Enguídanos&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20135827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Tejeda – La Loberuela loop from Narboneta&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to go road cycling in Enguídanos?
A: The region is generally pleasant for cycling during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, but the lush riverside areas and higher elevations offer some respite. The low traffic volume makes it enjoyable almost year-round, but always check local weather conditions.

Q: Is Enguídanos suitable for family-friendly road cycling?
A: Enguídanos's road cycling routes often involve significant elevation changes and are generally categorized as moderate to difficult, making them more suitable for experienced cyclists. While the low car traffic is a plus, families with younger children or those seeking very flat, easy rides might find the terrain challenging. Consider shorter, less hilly sections if cycling with family.

Q: Are there options for parking when starting a road cycling route?
A: Yes, Enguídanos and surrounding villages typically offer parking options. Many routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24538100" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertecillo de Yemeda – Enguídanos loop&lt;/a&gt;, start from recreational areas or villages where parking is available, such as Área recreativa La Playeta de Villora.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Enguídanos?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the tranquil environment due to low population density and minimal car traffic, as well as the stunning natural beauty of the Cabriel River Valley and the challenging yet rewarding mountainous terrain.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in scenic viewpoints. You can expect breathtaking views of river valleys, dramatic gorges, and the Embalse de Contreras. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3585363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertecillo de Yemeda&lt;/a&gt; is a notable mountain pass offering extensive regional views, and there are specific &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5398763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;views of Cabeza Moya&lt;/a&gt; to enjoy.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay in Enguídanos after a ride?
A: Yes, the village of Enguídanos itself, and other nearby towns, offer amenities for cyclists. You can find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options to refuel and relax after exploring the routes. The village is a picturesque starting and ending point for rides.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the famous Chorreras del Cabriel?
A: While primarily a destination for hiking and water activities, the roads leading to and around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5398723" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorreras del Cabriel&lt;/a&gt; offer incredibly scenic cycling with impressive views of the river's waterfalls and turquoise pools. Cycling in this area allows you to appreciate this natural monument from a different perspective.

Q: What makes the Cabriel River Valley special for road cyclists?
A: The Cabriel River Valley, a Biosphere Reserve, is special for its exceptionally clean, turquoise waters, dramatic geological formations, and lush riverside landscapes. Cycling along the roads that traverse the valley provides continuous stunning views and a sense of immersion in a pristine natural environment, making every ride visually rewarding.
tips: Beautiful town located at the foot of the Río Cabriel, before reaching the Contreras Reservoir. The center is located at the bottom of a valley in the middle of which is a hill where the old castle that bears the same name as the town is located. Surrounded by mountains and some river space for swimming.

Ascent by road with very nice views throughout the journey. When you reach the viewpoint, the views are even more impressive, you can see the Contreras Reservoir and the Cuchillares de las Hoces del Cabriel with their striking shapes.

The road between Cardenete and Enguídanos, in the Serranía Baja region of Cuenca, is one of the most beautiful stretches in this area. Although not officially designated as a "scenic road," the route is featured on hiking and cycling itineraries that highlight its stunning landscapes, rivers, gorges, mountains, and natural monuments.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route challenging?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is categorized as 'difficult' and a 'Hard road ride'. It covers approximately 134 kilometers with a significant elevation gain of 1,890 meters. Cyclists should have very good fitness and be prepared for a substantial day out.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: While primarily a road cycling route, some segments may be unpaved and difficult to ride, potentially requiring you to push your bike. Additionally, some paved descents feature very rough asphalt. This mix means the route demands a robust road bike or is perhaps better suited for a gravel bike.

faqs.question: What are the main sights along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers very nice views throughout the journey. Key highlights include the charming settlement of Enguídanos, spectacular vistas from the View of Embalse de Contreras and Cuchillares de las Hoces del Cabriel, and riding along the scenic Cardenete–Enguídanos Scenic Road. You'll also experience the stunning natural beauty of the Embalse de Contreras and the Natural Park of Hoces del Cabriel.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Enguídanos. While specific parking facilities aren't detailed, as a settlement, Enguídanos should offer public parking options suitable for cyclists looking to begin their ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Natural Park of Hoces del Cabriel, where this route is located, offers diverse landscapes. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for such a long and challenging ride, avoiding the extreme heat of summer or potential colder conditions in winter.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the Natural Park of Hoces del Cabriel. Generally, there are no entrance fees or special permits required for cycling on public roads and trails within the park. However, always check local regulations for any specific activities or protected areas you might encounter.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally allowed in natural parks, having a dog accompany you on a 134 km, difficult road ride with potentially unpaved sections is not practical or safe for the animal. It is not recommended to bring dogs on this specific cycling route.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the starting point in Enguídanos?
faqs.answer: Yes, Enguídanos is a settlement and serves as a good base for exploring the area. You should be able to find local cafes, pubs, and accommodation options suitable for cyclists before or after your ride.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling or hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several other official trails. You will briefly encounter parts of the Camino de Santiago de la Lana de Valencia, Ruta BTT de Cuenca, Camino de Santiago de la Lana de Alicante, and the Sendero de las Chorreras, among others.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this challenging ride?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length, elevation, and varied terrain (including rough and potentially unpaved sections), you should pack plenty of water and nutrition, a repair kit with spare tubes, appropriate clothing for changing weather, and a robust bike suitable for mixed surfaces. A GPS device is also highly recommended for navigation.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for a standard road bike with thin tires?
faqs.answer: Due to segments that may be unpaved and difficult, and descents with very rough asphalt, a standard road bike with thin tires might struggle. For a more comfortable and safer experience, a robust road bike with wider tires or a gravel bike would be a more suitable choice for this particular route.

waypoints.name: View of Embalse de Contreras and Cuchillares de las Hoces del Cabriel
